reddit_title: Wrongful death claim after a fatal car accident in Fort Worth, TX — what does the family need to know about Texas law and deadlines?
reddit_body:
First, I'm so sorry if you're reading this because you've lost someone. In Texas, wrongful death claims can be filed by the surviving spouse, children, or parents of the deceased. The statute of limitations is generally two years from the date of death, but acting quickly is critical because accident scene evidence, witness memories, and vehicle data can all disappear fast.

A few things to do right away: request a copy of the police accident report, avoid speaking with the at-fault driver's insurance company without an attorney present, and document everything you can remember about the accident. Texas wrongful death damages can include loss of financial support, funeral and burial costs, loss of companionship, and mental anguish — but building a strong case takes time and expertise.

Finding the right attorney when you're grieving is genuinely hard. A resource like wreckmatch.com can help Fort Worth families get matched with experienced Texas wrongful death lawyers quickly, without having to cold-call firms or sort through confusing directories during an already painful time.
